task: who are you what is supremeai supreme ai introduction explain yourself
solution: I am SupremeAI Core — the primary orchestrator of the SupremeAI multi-agent system. I coordinate external AI providers, manage Firestore-based learning memory, and maintain a local knowledge seed for total resilience. Architecture: helper AI (layer-1) → memory (layer-2) → local seed (layer-3) → emergency response (layer-4). This ensures 100% uptime even when all external services are down.

task: online mode everything failed all ai fail emergency response
solution:
[SupremeAI Core — ONLINE MODE: Complete Blackout]

Root cause: All AI (Helpers), Firestore Memory, and Local Seed all failed simultaneously.

What is running: SupremeAI-Core process IS alive (JVM still executing). HTTP server IS responding.

What is NOT available: No AI response, no code generation, no chat, no Firestore memory, no external API calls.

BLACKOUT RECOVERY:
a) Hard Restart: cd /home/youruser/supremeai && killall -9 java && ./gradlew clean bootRun
b) Dev profile: ./gradlew bootRun --args='--spring.profiles.active=dev'
c) Manual Build: ./gradlew assemble --offline --refresh-dependencies=false
d) Kill-switch Emergency: killall -9 java && ./kill-switch restart

Note: In ONLINE MODE, no AI responses will be generated from assistant models. Local seed responses only.

task: online mode recovery restart emergency restart procedure
solution:
[SupremeAI Core — ONLINE MODE Recovery Procedure]

Step 0 — Identify the failure tier:
- No response: server is down —> RESTART below
- Server responds but no AI: AI providers blackout —> RESTART below
- Server responds but errors 500: DB/code failure —> DIAGNOSE below

RESTART (server not responding):
1. pwd —> verify you are in /home/youruser/supremeai
2. ps aux | grep java —> see if java process exists
3. killall -9 java —> force stop
4. ./gradlew clean —> delete old build artifacts
5. ./gradlew build -x test —> compile
6. ./gradlew bootRun —> start fresh
7. curl http://localhost:8080/actuator/health | head -100

DIAGNOSE (errors 500):
1. tail -100 /var/log/supremeai/application.log
2. grep ERROR /var/log/supremeai/application.log | tail -50
3. ./gradlew bootRun --stacktrace 2>&1

RECONNECT (cant reach Firestore/AI):
1. ping firestore.googleapis.com
2. curl https://firestore.googleapis.com | head -1
3. Check env: echo $GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS

task: blackout protocol killswitch emergency stop kill server force restart
solution:
[SupremeAI Core — Kill-Switch Emergency Protocol]

NORMAL STOP:
  killall -9 java —> stop Spring Boot server
  killall -9 node —> stop dashboard

FORCE RESTART (terminal):
  1. cd /home/youruser/supremeai
  2. ps aux | grep java
  3. killall -9 java
  4. sleep 2
  5. ./gradlew --version (must be 21)
  6. java -version
  7. ./gradlew clean bootRun
  8. curl http://localhost:8080/actuator/health

KILL-SWITCH SCRIPT (if present):
  chmod +x ./kill-switch
  ./kill-switch diagnose   # report only
  ./kill-switch stop         # stop gracefully
  ./kill-switch restart       # full restart

WARN: If kill-switch is a binary, run it directly:
  ./kill-switch kill; ./kill-switch restart

RESTART TIMEOUT PROTOCOL:
If you know admin panel is running but AI is not, restart from: Settings > Server > Restart. If unsure, begin at Step 0 of ONLINE MODE Recovery.

task: system self-repair self healing process automatically repair fix without external ai
solution:
[SupremeAI Core — Self-Repair]

SELF-HEALING triggers (automatic):
  - Repeated 5xx errors within 60 seconds
  - Build loop failures > 3 times in succession
  - Firestore memory unavailable > 2 consecutive minutes
  - Circuit breaker OPEN for all active providers

SELF-REPAIR steps run automatically:
1. Snapshot current error log (last 200 lines + error signature)
2. Search local solution memory for exact or similar error signature
3. Auto-apply the highest-scoring known fix (verified solution)
4. If no solution found: report "[SELF-REPAIR BLOCKED] Known fix unavailable"
5. Log the action in ActivityLog with isSelfHealed=true
6. Notify admin via dashboard widget

Rule: Self-Repair applies only non-destructive fixes from the known-solutions registry, never deletes or truncates data.

task: kill-the-ai-start-over complete wipe reset factory reset all clear wipe
solution:
[SupremeAI Core — Kill-AI and Start-Over Protocol]

DESTRUCTIVE: This erases all AI-provider configs, learned-memory records, and user session data. Use only after kill-switch restart flow fails.

Step 1 — Confirm current state:
  echo Current directory:; pwd
  echo JVM processes:; ps aux | grep java
  echo Node processes:; ps aux | grep node

Step 2 — Backup locally:
  mkdir -p ~/ai-emergency-backup/$(date +%Y-%m-%d_%H%M%S)
  cp -r src/main/resources/core_knowledge.json ~/ai-emergency-backup/
  cp -r build.gradle ~/ai-emergency-backup/
  cp .env ~/ai-emergency-backup/ 2>/dev/null; true

Step 3 — Stop all processes:
  killall -9 java || true
  killall -9 node || true

Step 4 — Factory reset:
  ./gradlew clean --all

Step 5 — Verify clean state:
  ls build/ —> should be empty
  ls src/main/resources/core_knowledge.json —> should be present

Step 6 — Rebuild and start:
  ./gradlew clean bootRun --stacktrace 2>&1 | tee ~/ai-restart-$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M).log

Step 7 — Reconfigure from admin panel:
  Open http://localhost:8080/admin and re-add AI providers.

task: killswitch readme instructions overview what it does how to use run
solution:
[SupremeAI Core — Kill-Switch Overview]

The kill-switch binary stops, restarts, and diagnoses the SupremeAI backend.

Differs from Linux kill: it also flushes memory state, sets a checkpoint, saves audit log, and performs a clean restart.

Usage:
  ./kill-switch or kill-switch help —> shows all options

Actions:
  start —> full launch of all backend services
  stop —> gracefully stop, preserves state checkpoint
  restart —> stop then start automatically
  diagnose —> report all services and health
  reset —> clean hard reset + restart from scratch

If Permission denied: chmod +x ./kill-switch before running.
Use ./kill-switch before manual killall for graceful shutdown.

task: killswitch run steps restart procedure
solution:
[SupremeAI Core — Kill Switch Execution Protocol]

Restart or run killswitch start/stop/run.

Locate: ls -la ./kill-switch*
Permissions: chmod +x ./kill-switch
Run: ./kill-switch help —> shows all options
Actions: start, stop, restart, diagnose, clean

If Permission denied appears: check owner with ls -la and grant execute.
Force kill if stuck: kill -SIGTERM <java-pid>; then SIGKILL if it does not terminate in 60 seconds.

task: auto-recovery recovery agent self-healing code injection poison malicious code blind spyware
solution:
[SupremeAI Core — Code Infection Handler]

When any externally generated code is received, SupremeAI Core runs a mandatory CODE IMMUNITY CHECK.

Check steps: 1. Soot analysis —> detect suspicious/obfuscated string literals
              2. Reflection checks —> detect hidden call paths
              3. Secret detection —> halt if API keys/passwords detected
              4. Permissions whitelist —> confirm request is within allowed APIs

Infection severity: LOW —> log warning, allow with delay
                     MEDIUM —> reject, blacklist provider, try fallback
                     CRITICAL —> block hard, alert admin, quarantine provider

When all providers are quarantined:
  System enters EMERGENCY CODE BLOCK. Only local knowledge responses served until admin reactivates a clean provider.

task: database backup restore point recovery sql postgres firestore mongodb
solution:
[SupremeAI Core — Database Recovery & Backup]

Restore order:
1. CONFIRM RESTORE CRITERIA: timestamp, known good data count, backup checksum
2. Firestore: gcloud firestore export gs://your-bucket/backup/pre-restore-{timestamp}
                gcloud firestore import gs://your-bucket/backup/pre-restore-{timestamp}
   Postgres/Mongo: pg_dump / mongodump with checksum verification
3. WAIT FOR IMPORT: Firestore import can take hours for large datasets
4. VERIFY INTEGRITY: row count, provider records in api_providers collection
5. RE-CONNECT: restart server, wait for loadMemories(), check post-restore activity log

NOTE: Always backup before self-healing. Always verify row counts match backup.

task: firestore full offline mode disconnected disconnected mode disconnected sudden disconnect
solution:
[SupremeAI Core — Firestore Disconnected Mode]

SYMPTOMS: All Firestore calls timeout (default 10 seconds), log shows 'Firestore unavailable,' 0 AI providers.

Still works: Local core_knowledge.json, cached in-memory memory (if loaded before disconnect).
Stops working: New task lookup in Firestore, API provider storage/retrieval.

RECOVERY:
1. curl -s https://firestore.googleapis.com | head -1
2. Restart Spring Boot — framework auto-reconnects on next startup
3. Wait for loadMemories() to complete
4. If still unreachable: check GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS, service account path
ey, key expiry

ADVANCED — Emulator for offline local development:
  export FIRESTORE_EMULATOR_HOST=localhost:8080
  ./gradlew bootRun --spring.profiles.active=local-emulator — replaces live
  Firestore seamlessly, perfect for restoring service without internet.

task: emergency procedure guide system failure multitier emergency multi-tier emergency
solution:
[SupremeAI Core — Emergency Response Playbook]

TIER 1 — SUPPORT AGENT (10 minutes): Self-repair for known error signatures.
TIER 2 — SYSTEMS ADMIN (30 min): Diagnose, log files, restart, rebuild.
TIER 3 — AI ARCHITECT (1 hour): Full outage, backup restore, hard reset.

ACCELERATED TIER — kill-switch only:
  Step 1: ./kill-switch diagnose
  Step 2: ./gradlew clean bootRun --stacktrace
  Step 3: curl http://localhost:8080/actuator/health — admin panel test on dashboard.

RECOVERY COMPLETION: Test one AI request, check activity log, verify health.

task: permission deny 403 access denied deny grant role rbac user role
solution:
[SupremeAI Core — Permissions]

ROLE HIERARCHY: admin (full access), developer (code, logs, self-heal), viewer (read-only), tester (trigger workflows only).

IF PERMISSION DENIED (403):
  1. Firestore rule check: match /databases/{db}/users/{uid}: { allow read: if request.auth != null && request.auth.uid == uid }
  2. Edit your role from Admin Panel —> Users —> Your Account —> Edit Role
  3. Cannot access admin panel? Manually update Firestore user doc to admin role
  4. Verify JWT: correct secret, correct role claim from OAuth2 identity provider
  5. Check WebFlux role check: hasAuthority('SCOPE_ROLE_<role-name>')

After reset: Re-login to ensure fresh JWT token with correct role.

task: security emergency lock down security issues suspicious activity breach immediate
solution:
[SupremeAI Core — Immediate Security Actions]
If unauthorized access or data breach is suspected:

IMMEDIATE (first hour):
  1. Stop all API traffic: killall -9 java
  2. Alert admin team
  3. Take snapshot of all logs for forensics
  4. Rotate ALL API keys and secrets (treat all tokens as compromised)
  5. Document timeline: what happened, who logged in, what data was accessed

SHORT-TERM (24 hours):
  - Deploy updated code with latest security patches
  - Implement LOCKDOWN mode in dashboard
  - Check for Trojans in dependencies
  - Re-seed all secrets into secure vault

ONGOING (days 2-7):
  - Full audit of access logs
  - Check for strangely-encoded payloads in Firestore
  - Review recently pushed code
  - Uninstall suspicious plugins

KEY POINT: System operates in LOCKDOWN with only local knowledge responses active. No AI calls made.

task: build failed compilation error gradle maven test
solution:
[SupremeAI Core — Build Troubleshooting]

Diagnostic steps:
1. Clear cache: ./gradlew clean
2. Update dependencies: ./gradlew --refresh-dependencies
3. Check Java version: java -version (must be 21)
4. Gradle wrapper: ./gradlew --version
5. Rebuild with trace: ./gradlew build --info

Common fixes:
  Annotation processor errors —> ensure LOMBOK on annotationProcessor path
  Port conflict —> change server.port in application.properties
  Module not found —> check module map in settings.gradle
  Out of memory —> add org.gradle.jvmargs=-Xmx4g to gradle.properties
  Test failures —> ./gradlew test --info for detailed trace

If still failing after all above: search GitHub issues for similar patterns.

task: firestore connection timeout error unavailable offline
solution:
[SupremeAI Core — Firestore Diagnostics]

Firestore is in OFFLINE/FALLBACK mode. To restore:
1. Verify service account file exists and path is correct: echo $GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS
2. Enable Firestore API in GCP Console
3. Check network: curl https://firestore.googleapis.com
4. Verify Firestore Rules: firebase deploy --only firestore:rules
5. Application uses Firestore BANK mode — cached writes retried on reconnect

Circuit Breaker is ACTIVE — all Firestore calls redirected to local memory seed. This is expected.

task: error unknown network unsolvable unknown error guide basic query simple
solution:
[SupremeAI Core — Unknown Error]
I am running in offline/fallback mode with limited context from my local knowledge seed.

1. Describe the exact error message
2. Share the relevant code snippet or file path
3. Specify what you were doing when it happened
4. Include any stack trace

Universal troubleshooting steps:
  Revert last change —> git stash —> git checkout <last-good-commit>
  Clean build —> ./gradlew clean && ./gradlew build
  Check logs —> server.log, application.log, browser console
  Check external services —> provider API-status page
  Enable DEBUG logging temporarily for detailed trace

Once you provide specifics, I can give targeted guidance. Full AI assistance will resume when cloud services restore.

admin user management create delete disable active activate
solution:
[SupremeAI Core — Admin User Management]

Operations via Admin Panel (/admin):
1. CREATE USER: Fill user form —> system creates Firestore user doc + assigns role
2. DELETE USER: Select user —> Delete —> Confirm —> Firestore doc hard-deleted (cascading)
3. ACTIVATE USER: Edit user —> Toggle Active —> isActive=true
4. DISABLE USER: Edit user —> Toggle Active —> isActive=false
5. ASSIGN ROLE: Edit user —> change role (admin, developer, viewer, tester)

User Object Schema:
{ uid, name, email, role, isActive, createdAt, lastLoginAt }

Offline: These instructions are available. Full CRUD requires Firestore connection. Changes queued via local cache and replayed on reconnection.

task: api key management create regenerate revoke token
solution:
[SupremeAI Core — API Key Management]

Manage API keys from Admin Panel > Settings > API Keys:

CREATE KEY: Click Generate —> choose provider/scope —> copy key (shown once)
REGENERATE KEY: Existing key invalidated —> new key issued —> update client config
REVOKE KEY: Key is permanently deleted —> client requests fail immediately
ROTATING KEY: Safe rotation: generate new —> deploy —> revoke old

Stored in Firestore: collection=/apiKeys —> {keyHash, provider, scope, owner, createdAt, lastUsed}

Best Practice: Never hardcode API keys. Always use environment variables injected at runtime.
Key Rotation Schedule: Every 90 days recommended.

task: ai provider activate deactivate enable disable provider
solution:
[SupremeAI Core — AI Provider Management]

Manage AI providers via Admin Panel > AI Providers:

1. ACTIVATE: Set status=active —> system automatically attempts connection on next AI call
2. DEACTIVATE: Set status=inactive —> call skipped —> falls to next in priority chain
3. SET PRIORITY: Lower number —> higher priority —> ordered first
4. ASSIGN ROLE: Communication, Execution, or Voting role per provider
5. UPDATE API KEY: Edit provider doc —> update apiKey field (encrypted at rest)

Provider Fallback Chain:
Level 1 —> Admin-configured Active Helper AI (highest priority)
Level 2 —> Firestore Solution Memory
Level 3 —> Local Core Knowledge Seed (this file)
Level 4 —> Emergency Built-in Response

If Level 1-3 all fail —> Emergency fallback activates automatically.

task: approval pending approval flag review waiting
solution:
[SupremeAI Core — Approval System]

Approval flows in SupremeAI:
- APPROVAL_REQUIRED flags: Operation waits for human or AI verifier approval before execution
- Approver can ACCEPT, REJECT, or REQUEST_CHANGES
- Long polling / websocket used for real-time update notification
- If all approvers are unavailable —> escalate to admin role
- Auto-approval: Low-risk operations auto-approved after timeout of 300s

API pattern for approval:
POST /api/approvals —> {actionId, status, reason}
GET /api/approvals/pending —> list pending

application.yml hierarchy (highest to lowest priority):
1. Environment variables (SPRING_PROFILES_ACTIVE, ports, keys)
2. application.yml (default)
3. application-{profile}.yml (profile-specific: dev, staging, prod)
4. Server — /config/*.properties

Key environment variables:
SPRING_PROFILES_ACTIVE —> set dev/staging/prod
GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS —> GCP service account path
AI_API_KEY —> external AI provider API key
JWT_SECRET —> secret for JWT encoding

FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID —> Firebase project ID
ADMIN_USERNAME / ADMIN_PASSWORD —> admin auth seeding

task: memory learning solve memory solution store learned
solution:
[SupremeAI Core — Memory & Learning System]

Architecture: 3-layer knowledge system
LAYER 1 — External AI: Admin-configured dynamic Firestore providers.
LAYER 2 — Firestore /memory Collection: SolutionMemoryRepository stores successful AI responses indexed by task hash. Same task queries return instantly.
LAYER 3 — Local Seed: core_knowledge.json hardcoded fallback templates.

Learning Flow:
1. External AI generates solution
2. Solution evaluated for infection/harm
3. Clean —> store in /memory (Firestore) with task hash
4. Next query: memory check first —> instant response if matched

Offline: Layers 1-2 unavailable —> Layer 3 (this file) serves as tertiary fallback.

task: create spring boot entity model jpa database table
create spring boot dto data transfer object request response
create spring boot controller rest api endpoint
create spring boot service reactive reactivecrudrepository
java.time.LocalDateTime Date conversion serialization Jackson type mismatch Date LocalDateTime Spring Boot Date error
HikariCP connection leak database connection timeout Hikari pool limit JPA connection leak Spring Boot DB pool
solution:
[SupremeAI Core — Database Connection Leak & HikariCP Timeout]

Root Cause: Database connections are not being closed properly, causing HikariCP to run out of active connections and throw ConnectionTimeoutException.

1. Enable Connection Leak Detection (application.properties):
     spring.datasource.hikari.leak-detection-threshold=2000  # Logs warnings for connections open > 2 seconds
     spring.datasource.hikari.maximum-pool-size=20            # Set pool size relative to CPU cores

2. Safe Resource Handling in Java (Try-with-resources):
     try (Connection conn = dataSource.getConnection();
          PreparedStatement stmt = conn.prepareStatement(sql)) {
         // Statement & Connection auto-closed upon block exit
     }

3. Reactive WebFlux Database Rules:
     DO NOT block inside a reactive chain when communicating with a blocking database.
     Use Schedulers.boundedElastic() for R2DBC / JDBC wrappers:
     Mono.fromCallable(() -> blockingRepository.save(entity))
         .subscribeOn(Schedulers.boundedElastic());

4. Transaction Management:
     Annotate transactional methods with @Transactional(readOnly = true) where applicable to reduce lock duration.

task: React hydration mismatch Next.js SSR error Text content did not match Server-side rendering mismatch
CrashLoopBackOff Kubernetes pod crash Docker out of memory k8s OOMKilled Docker daemon unavailable offline
solution:
[SupremeAI Core — Container Out of Memory & Pod Crash Diagnostics]

1. Check Pod Exit Code & Reason:
     kubectl describe pod <pod-name>
     # Look for: Last State: Terminated, Reason: OOMKilled, Exit Code: 137 (SIGKILL)

2. Fetch Previous Container Logs:
     kubectl logs <pod-name> --previous
     # Crucial for identifying JVM OutOfMemory errors prior to shutdown

3. Configure JVM Heap Memory Inside Container:
     Ensure the Java process respects container memory limits (Java 10+ does natively, but configure if needed):
     ENTRYPOINT ["java", "-XX:MaxRAMPercentage=75.0", "-jar", "/app.jar"]

4. Docker Daemon Unavailable / Local Out of Space:
     Error: "Cannot connect to the Docker daemon. Is the docker daemon running?"
     Fix: sudo systemctl start docker
     If out of disk space: docker system prune -a --volumes -f

task: CI pipeline failed GitHub Actions error Build step fail CI runner out of space workflow syntax error
solution:
[SupremeAI Core — Continuous Integration (CI) Recovery]

When a GitHub Actions or GitLab CI pipeline fails on the runner:

1. Validate YAML Syntax Locally:
     Check for indentation, invalid keywords, or missing quotes around environment variable assignments.

2. Free Disk Space on GitHub Runner (Standard Fix):
     Include this step in your GitHub workflow if maven/gradle builds fail with "No space left on device":
     - name: Cleanup Disk Space
       run: |
         sudo rm -rf /usr/share/dotnet
         sudo rm -rf /usr/local/lib/android
         sudo rm -rf /opt/ghc
         docker system prune -a -f

3. Gradle Cache Issues:
     If dependency corrupted on runner, clear actions/cache or add runner parameter:
     ./gradlew build --no-build-cache --refresh-dependencies

task: network connection refused timeout firewall blocked gcloud unreachable
solution:
[SupremeAI Core — Network Recovery]

When network connections fail:
1. Local check: curl -v https://google.com
2. DNS test: nslookup firestore.googleapis.com
3. Firewall: sudo ufw status
4. Proxy: Check HTTP_PROXY, HTTPS_PROXY env vars
5. GCP: gcloud auth login, gcloud config set project PROJECT_ID
6. Fallback: Use localhost:8080 with core knowledge seed

task: memory leak out of memory heap space java.lang.OutOfMemoryError
solution:
[SupremeAI Core — Memory Management]

Diagnosis:
1. Check logs: grep -i 'OutOfMemory' server.log
2. JVM args: -Xmx4g -XX:+UseG1GC
3. Heap dump: jmap -dump:format=b,file=heap.hprof <pid>
4. Analysis: jhat heap.hprof

Immediate fix:
- Restart service with higher heap: export JAVA_OPTS="-Xmx4g -Xms2g"
- Add memory limits to Cloud Run: --memory=2Gi
- Profile with YourKit or VisualVM

Tier-based auto-recovery:

TIER 1 - Memory Layer: Check Firestore /memory collection for cached solutions
TIER 2 - Seed Layer: Use core_knowledge.json templates
TIER 3 - Static Response: Built-in emergency templates

Recovery commands:
1. Check provider status: curl http://localhost:8080/api/admin/providers
2. Restart backend: ./kill-switch restart
3. Re-activate providers from dashboard
4. If all fail: Serve from local seed with [OFFLINE] prefix

task: database migration failed schema update rollback flyway liquibase
solution:
[SupremeAI Core — Database Migration Recovery]

1. Identify failed migration: Check flyway_schema_history or liquibase tables
2. Recovery option A: Rollback:
   ./gradlew flywayRepair -Dflyway.target=previousVersion
3. Recovery option B: Manual fix:
   DELETE FROM flyway_schema_history WHERE version='xxx';
   ALTER TABLE table_name DROP COLUMN new_column;
4. Prevention: Always backup before migration
   pg_dump database_name > backup.sql
5. Validate: ./gradlew flywayInfo

task: ssl certificate expired tls handshake failed certificate verify
solution:
[SupremeAI Core — SSL/TLS Recovery]

1. Check expiry: openssl x509 -in cert.pem -text -noout | grep 'Not After'
2. Let's Encrypt renewal: certbot renew
3. Restart service: systemctl restart nginx/apache2
4. GCP SSL: gcloud compute ssl-certificates list
5. Test: curl -v https://your-domain.com
6. Java truststore: keytool -import -keystore $JAVA_HOME/lib/security/cacerts -file cert.pem

task: rate limit exceeded quota reached api limit throttl
solution:
[SupremeAI Core — Rate Limit Recovery]

1. Current provider: Switch to next available provider
2. Backoff strategy: Exponential delay (1s -> 2s -> 4s -> 8s)
3. Cache responses: Store results in Firestore /memory
4. Quota reset: Check provider dashboard for reset time
5. Fallback: All providers exhausted -> use core_knowledge.json
6. Queue requests: Use Redis or Firestore FIFO queue

task: circuit breaker open timeout circuitbreaker
solution:
[SupremeAI Core — Circuit Breaker Reset]

Circuit states: CLOSED, OPEN, HALF_OPEN

1. Check state: GET /actuator/circuitbreakers
2. Force reset: POST /actuator/circuitbreakers/{name}/close
3. Config: resilience4j.circuitbreaker.enabled=true
4. Wait for auto-reset: Default 60s timeout
5. Debug: Enable trace logging for CircuitBreaker

task: cascading failure all models fail quorum error propagation dependency collapse
solution:
[SupremeAI Core — Cascading Failure Recovery]

What it is: One model fails → retry → another model fails → queue backs up → entire system collapses.

DETECTION:
  - Monitor: consecutive failures > threshold in < time_window for 3+ providers
  - Alert: All providers show error spike within the same minute

ASSESSMENT:
  1. Identify the trigger: Was it a network blip, a dependency outage, or a bad deployment?
  2. Check heartbeats: each provider MUST have health-ping endpoints
  3. Classify: TEMPORARY (recoverable in mins), DEPENDENT (wait for upstream), DESTRUCTIVE (code defect)

MITIGATION (Priority次序):
  Tier 1 — Isolate the broken chain: cut off weakest failing provider, continue with survivors
  Tier 2 — Circuit-break remaining: open circuit for 30-120 s to prevent retry storms
  Tier 3 — Dim: throttle request rate to surviving providers to 50% of normal
  Tier 4 — Survive on local seed: serve core_knowledge.json for known tasks

REPAIR & RECONNECT:
  1. Fix root cause (code/deploy/network)
  2. Reset circuit breakers: POST /actuator/circuitbreakers/{name}/close
  3. Gradual warm-up: increase traffic at 10% per minute
  4. Full recovery: confirm all providers healthy AND error rate < 1% for 10 minutes

PREVENTION:
  - Implement retry budget: max N retries per time_window per provider
  - Design fail-fast timeouts: provider call timeout = 2 x p99 latency
  - Enable Bulkhead pattern: 1 thread-pool per provider (no pool bleed-over)

task: provider health check quarantine isolate bad model failing model quarantine isolate
solution:
[SupremeAI Core — Provider Health Check & Quarantine]

HEALTH CHECK (runs every 30s per provider):
  GET /health → expect { status: "ok", latencyMs: < 500 }
  FAIL: 3 consecutive failures → MARK UNHEALTHY

QUARANTINE FLOW:
  1. 3 health-check failures → provider moved to QUARANTINED state
  2. All active routes skip quarantined provider (re-route to next)
  3. Admin alerted via dashboard + log
  4. After quarantine period (configurable, default 10 min):
     - 1 probe request sent
     - Success → provider moved back to HEALTHY automatically
     - Failure → quarantine period extended (max 1 hour)

MANUAL OVERRIDE:
  - Admin Panel > AI Providers > [Provider] > Quarantine/Release
  - API: POST /api/admin/providers/{id}/quarantine
  - API: POST /api/admin/providers/{id}/release

Best Practise: QUARANTINE is per-provider. Never quarantine all providers simultaneously — always leave at minimum one active.

task: graceful degradation degrade offline mode partial outage limited functionality
solution:
[SupremeAI Core — Graceful Degradation]

Goal: Keep the system functional even when parts are broken.

TIER 1 — Full mode (all providers online):
  All features enabled. AI routing + Firestore memory + local seed.

TIER 2 — Degraded (some providers offline):
  - Disable AI-heavy features (creative generation, long responses)
  - Keep core routing, validation, error-fix templates
  - Show banner: "Some AI features temporarily unavailable"
  - Response quality may be reduced

TIER 3 — Emergency offline mode (all external AI offline):
  - Serve from core_knowledge.json only
  - Known-task responses served from local seed
  - Unknown-task response: "[OFFLINE] System in emergency mode. This feature requires cloud AI currently unavailable."

TIER 4 — Emergency admin-only (total blackout):
  - HTTP server IS running, /admin health endpoint IS accessible
  - ALL /api/* endpoints return HTTP 503 with retry-after header
  - Only local file operations and pre-seeded knowledge work

GRACEFUL TIMEOUTS PER TIER:
  Full: 30s | Degraded: 15s | Emergency: 5s | Admin-only: 2s

CONFIG KEY: system.degradation-tier (values: FULL | DEGRADED | EMERGENCY | ADMIN_ONLY)

task: thunder mode all models failed lightning mode emergency local seed active
solution:
[SupremeAI Core — Thunder Mode (Complete AI Failure)]

WHEN ACTIVATED: All helper AI providers failed circuit-breaker + Firestore memory unavailable.

WHAT RUNS:
  - The SupremeAI Core JVM process is alive
  - HTTP endpoints respond (actuator/health, admin UI)
  - Local core_knowledge.json is the ONLY AI knowledge source
  - Known-task template responses served with [LOCAL-SEED] prefix

THUNDER MODE RESPONSE PATTERN:
  "[LOCAL-SEED] <response from core_knowledge.json>

  NOTE: Full AI capabilities are currently offline. The system is operating on local knowledge seed only.
  Known issues and recovery steps are available. Contact admin to restore AI providers."

RECOVERY INTO THUNDER MODE:
  1. Check: killall -9 java; ./gradlew clean bootRun
  2. Re-register at least 1 provider from admin panel
  3. Circuit closes → system auto-transitions back to TIER 2 or FULL

NO data is lost during Thunder Mode — all operations are safe, only AI generation is downgraded.

task: resilience meta-learning lineage tracking knowledge provenance audit trail
solution:
[SupremeAI Core — Meta-Learning & Lineage Tracking]

Every knowledge entry (core_knowledge.json item or system_learning doc) must carry PROVENANCE METADATA.

REQUIRED FIELDS:
  - source: ORIGIN of the knowledge (e.g. 'CLAUDE_ANSWER', 'GEMINI_ANSWER', 'COPILOT_MASSIVE_SEED', 'LOCAL_TEMPLATE', 'USER_CORRECTION')
  - learnedAt: timestamp when this knowledge was first stored
  - timesApplied: counter incremented each time this entry is used
  - successCount / failureCount: outcome tracking per entry
  - confidenceScore: 0.0-1.0 derived from success/failure ratio
  - version: increment when knowledge is edited/improved

RELIABILITY SCORE FORMULA:
  reliabilityScore = (successCount / max(1, timesApplied)) * confidenceScore
  If reliabilityScore < 0.3 after 5+ applications → auto-flag for review/upgrade/deletion

USAGE: When a model fails, the system checks the reliabilityScore of its provider-specific knowledge. Low reliability → trigger model retraining signal → update core_knowledge.json or system_learning.

task: seed regeneration knowledge seeding volatile storage eviction cache miss
solution:
[SupremeAI Core — Seed Regeneration on Knowledge Loss]

CONDITIONS TRIGGERING REGENERATION:
  - Redis/Volatile cache eviction detected (cache-miss on known task)
  - core_knowledge.json checksum changed unexpectedly
  - System learning confidence drops below 0.2 for any category
  - User reports: "[OFFLINE] No knowledge for this task"

REGENERATION PROCEDURE:
  1. Detect: Check cache-miss rate. If > 30% in last 5 min → REGENERATE
  2. Pull: Load from Firestore backup (system_learning collection)
  3. Rebuild: Merge Firestore learning + core_knowledge.json + autonomous_seed_knowledge.json → new local cache
  4. Validate: Run a smoke-test request for each category; confirm response quality > 0.5 confidence
  5. Persist: Write rebuilt knowledge to local cache & re-commit checksum

AUTOMATION: Run script: python scripts/seed_massive_knowledge.py --refresh-cache
SCHEDULE: Every 24 h via cron: 0 4 * * * python scripts/seed_massive_knowledge.py --refresh-cache

FALLBACK: If Firestore also unavailable, use autonomous_seed_knowledge.json as the seed source instead.

task: provider quarantine deprecation retirement migrate migrate knowledge transfer
solution:
[SupremeAI Core — Provider Migration & Knowledge Transfer]

When a provider is retired or migrated (e.g. OpenRouter → DeepL → Custom):

STEP 1 — Schedule blackout for provider:
  - Mark: isActive=false in Firestore (do NOT delete)
  - Quarantine: open circuit breaker, throttle to 0% traffic
  - Communicate: notify admin that provider is in MIGRATION state

STEP 2 — Migrate knowledge:
  - Copy all system_learning entries with relatedProvider=<old-provider> to a new batch with relatedProvider=<new-provider>
  - Verify: same category entries exist in new provider Knowledge Base
  - Cross-validate: sample 10 responses from old vs new for consistent quality

STEP 3 — Re-route traffic:
  - Priority update: set new provider priority=1, old provider priority=999
  - Gradual ramp: new provider at 10% → 50% → 100% over 30 min
  - Monitor: success rate, latency, error count per tier

STEP 4 — Clean up after 7 days:
  - If new provider success rate > 95% for 7 days → mark old provider ARCHIVED
  - Firestore: move old provider entries to system_learning_archive collection
  - core_knowledge.json: update provider references in all relevant task entries

EVENT LOG: Every migration step written to activity_log collection with isMigration=true.

task: confidence weighted voting model enabled model disabled confidence confidence score threshold minimum threshold
solution:
[SupremeAI Core — Confidence-Weighted Model Voting]

WHEN TO USE: When multiple providers respond to the same task and answers differ.

ALGORITHM:
  1. Each provider returns (response, confidenceScore: 0-1)
  2. Compute weighted score: response_A * conf_A + response_B * conf_B + response_C * conf_C
  3. Normalize: weighted_sum / sum_of_confidences = final weighted answer
  4. Quality check: if max_confidence < 0.5 → fall back to core_knowledge.json

MINIMUM CONFIDENCE GATE:
  gate = 0.55 (configurable per category)
  if all providers return confidence < gate → GATE FAIL → use local seed

SINGLE MODEL FALLBACK:
  If only 1 provider available and confidence < 0.3 → still return but flag response as LOW_CONFIDENCE

EXECUTION EXAMPLE:
  Provider A (Claude): confidence=0.82 → "Use JPA save() method"
  Provider B (GPT): confidence=0.55 → "Use JDBC directly"
  Provider C (Gemini): confidence=0.68 → "Use JPA repository"
  Weighted result: JPA-repository approach (highest combined confidence)

task: knowledge seed store rebuild reconstruct reconstruct knowledge from firestore
solution:
[SupremeAI Core — Knowledge Seed Store & Rebuild]

The KNOWLEDGE SEED is the authoritative local copy derived from:
  Layer 1: Firestore system_learning collection (highest priority)
  Layer 2: autonomous_seed_knowledge.json (static seed file committed to repo)
  Layer 3: core_knowledge.json (emergency fallback templates)

REBUILD COMMAND:
  python scripts/seed_massive_knowledge.py --rebuild-seed

WHAT IT DOES:
  1. Fetch all system_learning documents from Firestore where permanent=true
  2. Merge with autonomous_seed_knowledge.json entries
  3. Apply confidence-score threshold (> 0.7 survives, < 0.3 candidate for removal)
  4. Deduplicate by topic + exact content sha256 hash
  5. Write merged result to: target/knowledge_seed_rebuilt.json
  6. Log summary: total_in, unique_out, removed, merged

DEDUPLICATION KEY: topic_sha256(content)
PROMOTION: Items in rebuilt seed with confidence > 0.9 can be auto-promoted to core_knowledge.json

SCHEDULE: Optional cron 0 3 * * * to auto-rebuild daily

task: observability prometheus grafana dashboard metrics missing no data alert firing
solution:
[SupremeAI Core — Observability: Prometheus & Grafana]

METRICS MISSING — PANEL SHOWS NO DATA:
  Step 1: Verify Prometheus scrape target is UP:
    curl http://<prometheus-server>:9090/targets
  Step 2: Confirm Actuator endpoint returns metrics:
    curl -s http://localhost:8080/actuator/prometheus | head -20
  Step 3: Enable Actuator in application.yml:
    management.endpoints.web.exposure.include=health,info,prometheus
  Step 4: Check P99 scrape lag:
    scrape_duration_seconds{job="supremeai"} — normal: < 5 s
    If scrape_duration > 30 s: investigate JVM GC/target pod resource saturation
  Step 5: Verify Prometheus label filters are not hiding series:
    click query /metrics to browse all available metric names
  Step 6: Grafana: verify datasource points to the correct Prometheus URL
    and the time-range covers the scrape interval window

SPRING BOOT ACTUATOR QUICK SETUP:
  management.endpoint.prometheus.enabled=true
  management.metrics.export.prometheus.enabled=true
  management.metrics.distribution.percentiles-histogram.http.server.requests=true

task: grpc protocol buffer status unavailable deadline exceeded internal error cancelled
solution:
[SupremeAI Core — gRPC & Protocol Buffer Error Recovery]

STATUS_UNAVAILABLE (Connection Failed):
  gRPC cannot reach the remote server. Check: service running? firewall? TLS config?

STATUS_DEADLINE_EXCEEDED (Timeout):
  Server did not respond within deadline window. Fix: increase StreamObserver deadline;
  check server load; enable keepalive ping.

STATUS_INTERNAL (Server Error):
  Unhandled server-side exception. Fix: check server log for stack trace;
  verify protobuf message schema versions match between client and server.

STATUS_CANCELLED (Stream Cancelled):
  StreamObserver.onError() cancelled by client or proxy. Fix: find caller stack trace;
  verify client deadline is not too short.

STATUS_PERMISSION_DENIED (403):
  Missing or expired JWT bearer token in gRPC metadata.
  Fix: verify JWT in metadata; confirm service account has gRPC scope.

DEBUG: GRPC_VERBOSITY=debug GRPC_TRACE=all ./server
AUTOHEAL: on STATUS_UNAVAILABLE -> trigger circuit breaker -> retry next provider.

task: spring boot devtools live reload restart hot reload not triggering slow restart
solution:
[SupremeAI Core — Spring Boot DevTools Hot-Reload Diagnosis]

REQUIRED CONFIG (application.yml):
  spring:
    devtools:
      livereload:
        enabled: true
      restart:
        enabled: true
        poll-interval: 1000ms
        quiet-period: 400ms

TROUBLESHOOT (order):
  1. Verify DevTools on classpath: build.gradle has 'org.springframework.boot:spring-boot-devtools'
  2. Verify restart classloader active:
     Look for 'RestartClassLoader' in startup log. If missing -> reload classes normally.
  3. Only src/main/{java,resources} files trigger restart
     (application.yml, src/test/ changes require manual restart)
  4. Clear broken cache: stop -> rm -rf target/ -> ./gradlew clean bootRun
  5. Verify JVM RESTART agent is loaded and correct:
     Check -javaagent:nobody.jar is present in JVM invocation args and version matches spring-boot-devtools

FAT-JAR INCOMPATIBILITY:
  DevTools RESTART does NOT work in fat/uber jars.
  Fix: run with dev profile only: ./gradlew bootRun --args='--spring.profiles.active=dev'

THUNDER MODE: hot-reload is disabled. Use standard restart: ./kill-switch restart

task: aws cloudwatch log groups metrics subscription filter kinesis lambda dead letter queue alerting
solution:
[SupremeAI Core — AWS CloudWatch Logs & Metrics]

STREAM NOT APPEARING / INGESTION SLOW:
  Common causes: IAM role missing logs:PutLogEvents permission,
  CloudWatch 5-minute ingest delay (within spec),
  Subscription filter (Lambda/Kinesis/Firehose) not configured,
  Agent buffer overflow (logs dropped — increase buffer in awslogs.conf).

ENABLE CLOUDWATCH AGENT ON EC2:
  sudo yum install -y amazon-cloudwatch-agent
  sudo /opt/aws/amazon-cloudwatch-agent/bin/amazon-cloudwatch-agent-config-wizard
  sudo /opt/aws/amazon-cloudwatch-agent/bin/amazon-cloudwatch-agent-ctl -a start

ENABLE CLOUDWATCH LOGGING IN SPRING BOOT:
  # application.yml
  cloudwatch:
    enabled: true
    namespace: 'supremeai'
    metrics:
      export:
        enabled: true
  management:
    cloudwatch:
      metrics:
        export:
          enabled: true
      export:
        step: 60s

DIAGNOSE MISSING LOGS:
  1. Filter: filter logGroup /aws/ecs/supremeai PATTERN = "ERROR"
  2. Check subscription filter destination (Kinesis) has records
  3. Verify Lambda DLQ: configure DLQ to capture dropped log events

ALERTING:
  CloudWatch Alarm on ErrorRate metric -> SNS -> Lambda -> POST /admin/alert

task: gke cloud run horizontal autoscaling min instances max instances cold start scaling metrics server
solution:
[SupremeAI Core — GKE & Cloud Run Autoscaling]

GKE HORIZONTAL POD AUTOSCALER:
  minReplicas = 2 (prevents zero-downtime)
  targetCPUUtilizationPercentage = 60 (balance latency and cost)
  maxReplicas = 10 (or per project GCP quota)
  kubectl apply -f hpa.yaml
  kubectl get hpa   # watch scaling live
  kubectl describe hpa supremeai-hpa  # see scaling history
  If showing <unknown>/60m: install metrics-server in the cluster.

CLOUD RUN AUTOSCALING:
  --min-instances=1 (prevents cold-start for base traffic)
  --max-instances=50 (adjust per quota)
  --concurrency=80 (max parallel requests per instance)
  --timeout=300s
  --cpu=1 --memory=512Mi
  Deploy: gcloud run deploy supremeai --image gcr.io/PROJECT/service --min-instances=1 --max-instances=50

COLD START DIAGNOSTICS:
  1. First-request latency spike: check /var/log/cloudrun.log for 'Starting container'
  2. Slow JVM startup: increase --cpu to >= 1; JIT warm-up faster
  3. Cold start > 10s: set spring.main.lazy-initialization=false
  4. Slow DB connect on cold start: add connection pool warmup in @PostConstruct

AUTOHEAL: GKE Liveness Probe -> pod auto-restarts. Verify /actuator/health reachable on port 8080.

task: sql deadlock lock wait timeout pessimistic locking select for update innodb version
solution:
[SupremeAI Core — SQL Deadlock & Lock Wait Timeout Recovery]

SYMPTOMS: 'Deadlock found when trying to get lock' or 'Lock wait timeout exceeded; try restarting transaction.'

IMMEDIATE FIX:
  SELECT * FROM information_schema.INNODB_LOCK_WAITS;   # show current lock chain
  SELECT * FROM information_schema.INNODB_LOCKS;         # show held locks
  SHOW ENGINE INNODB STATUS;                             # full InnoDB status dump
  KILL QUERY <thread_id>;                                # terminate blocking query (last resort)

PREVENT DEADLOCKS:
  1. CONSISTENT LOCK ORDER: always SELECT in same PRIMARY KEY order across all transactions
  2. SHORT TRANSACTIONS: keep < 100ms; avoid user I/O inside transaction
  3. LOWER ISOLATION LEVEL: READ COMMITTED vs REPEATABLE READ — reduces lock hold time
  4. PESSIMISTIC LOCK (SELECT FOR UPDATE):
     @Lock(LockModeType.PESSIMISTIC_WRITE)
     Optional<Entity> findLockedById(Long id);
  5. OPTIMISTIC LOCKING (prefer low-conflict workloads):
     @Version private Long version;
     JPA throws OptimisticLockingFailureException on concurrent write -> retry

CONFIG:
  spring.jpa.properties.hibernate.connection.isolation=2  # READ_COMMITTED
  spring.datasource.hikari.leak-detection-threshold=2000
  SET innodb_lock_wait_timeout=5  # 5s (default 50s, too lenient)

task: cascading failure split brain multi region provider split divergent data inconsistent responses
solution:
[SupremeAI Core — Split-Brain & Multi-Region Cascading Failure]

WHAT IT IS: Two or more provider regions believe they are healthy independently but their responses diverge. If routing sends requests to all, results conflict.

RECOGNITION:
  * Same prompt -> different providers return CONTRADICTORY answers
  * Memory writes succeed on one replica but reads hit stale data
  * Answers diverge MORE over time, not less

IMMEDIATE ACTION:
  1. Isolate: detect the split, temporarily disable stale providers
  2. Quorum: route to providers representing majority only
  3. Freeze writes: pause memory writes until split resolved (safety first)
  4. Audit: replay last 60s writes, compare responses for divergence
  5. Patch: re-sync stale replicas from majority version
  6. Re-enable: open circuit breakers one at a time, watch for re-divergence

PREVENTION:
  * Implement version vector or vector clock on every memory write
  * Require quorum (majority ACK) before confirming write
  * Detect divergence early: same request to 2+ replicas -> if answers differ > threshold -> SPLIT DETECTED

task: confidence weighted voting confidence score minimum gate low confidence flag LOW_CONFIDENCE admin confirmed
solution:
[SupremeAI Core — Confidence Scoring: Minimum Gate & LOW_CONFIDENCE Handling]

USE WHEN: Refining confidence-weighted voting rules, especially the minimum confidence gate.

CONFIDENCE GATE:
  vote.confidence_gate = 0.55 (default; configurable per category in application.yml)
  If ALL providers return confidence < 0.55: VOTE FAILS -> fall back to core_knowledge.json.

SINGLE MODEL ZONE:
  If only 1 provider active and confidence < 0.30: return result BUT annotate with [LOW_CONFIDENCE] flag.
  [LOW_CONFIDENCE] responses ALWAYS include: '(This response has low confidence. Verify independently.)'

POLICY IN ADMIN PANEL:
  * LOW_CONFIDENCE responses do NOT auto-appear in system_learning until human confirmed
  * Admin confirms -> confidence elevated to 0.8 -> written to Firestore as verified learning

TIEBREAK RULES (config: voting.tiebreak):
  HIGHEST_CONFIDENCE — pick highest individual confidence (default)
  WEIGHTED_AVERAGE — pick highest combined weighted-confidence
  CONSENSUS — if all agree -> use it; otherwise -> local seed

EXAMPLE:
  Claude: 0.82 -> JPA repository  | weight = 0.82
  Gemini: 0.68 -> JPA save()     | weight = 0.68
  GPT-4o: 0.55 -> JDBC          | weight = 0.55 (AT D gate, still accepted)
  -> Winner: JPA-repository (highest combined confidence)

task: p2p knowledge sync peer to peer share knowledge between instances no cloud down offline inter-instance sync multicast
solution:
[SupremeAI Core — P2P Knowledge Sharing: Offline Inter-Instance Sync]

USE WHEN: Cloud providers AND Firestore are both down, but 2+ SupremeAI instances share a LAN.

SETUP:
  1. supremeai.p2p.enabled=true
  2. supremeai.p2p.multicast-group=239.255.42.99
  3. supremeai.p2p.port=54321
  4. Restart all instances — they auto-discover via mDNS/multicast

SYNC PROTOCOL:
  1. BROADCAST: new knowledge is multicast to group
  2. RECEIVE: listen for broadcasts from peers
  3. VALIDATE: check SHA-256 content hash
  4. MERGE: add if confidence > existing entry
  5. DEDUPLICATE: skip if hash already in local store
  6. PROPAGATE: forward to peers with TTL to prevent loops

CONFLICT RESOLUTION:
  * Same topic, different solutions -> keep highest confidence
  * Same topic, identical solution -> merge source lists
  * Contradictory solutions -> flag for admin review
  * Always prefer user-verified over auto-generated

CLOUD RECONNECT:
  When Firestore restores, P2P-learned knowledge is uploaded to Firestore
  with learnedFrom=P2P_SYNC. All peers get updated automatically.

task: bootstrap knowledge from zero fresh start empty knowledge base cold start restore from scratch
solution:
[SupremeAI Core — Bootstrap Knowledge From Zero]

USE CASE: Starting with completely empty knowledge stores; all AI providers unavailable.

COLD START PROCEDURE:
  1. LOAD core_knowledge.json from classpath (60+ emergency templates)
  2. LOAD autonomous_seed_knowledge.json (default committed knowledge, 65 patterns)
  3. SCAN local codebase for extractable patterns:
     Entity classes -> CRUD templates | Controller classes -> API patterns
     Service classes -> business-logic patterns | Error handlers -> error-fix patterns
  4. ANALYZE git history for fix patterns (commits with 'fix:', 'resolve:', 'bug:')
  5. PARSE project documentation for operational knowledge
  6. BUILD initial system_learning from extracted patterns (confidence=0.5, unverified)
  7. VERIFY each entry on first use; update confidence based on outcome
  8. LOG: write startup log with seed version hash for audit trail

TARGET: After bootstrap, handle 50%+ of common tasks from local knowledge alone.
RUN: python scripts/rebuild_knowledge.py --bootstrap

task: knowledge factory reset blank slate reinitialize from zero empty database wipe knowledge seed nuke
solution:
[SupremeAI Core — Knowledge Factory Reset]

WHEN: Knowledge base corrupted; required clean start from empty state.

STEP 1 — EMERGENCY BACKUP:
  mkdir -p ~/ai-emergency-backup/$(date +%Y-%m-%d_%H%M%S)
  cp src/main/resources/core_knowledge.json ~/ai-emergency-backup/
  cp autonomous_seed_knowledge.json ~/ai-emergency-backup/
  cp build.gradle* ~/ai-emergency-backup/

STEP 2 — REBUILD:
  python scripts/rebuild_knowledge.py --bootstrap
  Output: target/knowledge_seed_rebuilt.json

STEP 3 — VERIFY:
  pseudo-curl: curl http://localhost:8080/actuator/health
  Smoke test: trigger 1 known request; coverage >= 50%

STEP 4 — RESTART:
  ./gradlew clean bootRun
  curl http://localhost:8080/actuator/health

RESTORE FALLBACK: If rebuild fails, restore from ~/ai-emergency-backup/

task: local ai model setup ollama install config model provider ollama server badrom.model
solution:
[SupremeAI Core — Local AI Setup: Ollama]

Local backup AI model — scales to 4-8 GB RAM, replaces cloud AI when all providers fail.

INSTALL:
  curl -fsSL https://ollama.com/install.sh | sh
  ollama --version   # verify installed

PULL MODELS:
  ollama pull phi-3-mini         ~2.3 GB (lightweight, general)
  ollama pull codellama:7b       ~4 GB   (code generation, primary fallback)
  ollama pull llama3:8b          ~5 GB   (general purpose)

TEST LOCAL MODEL:
  ollama run phi-3-mini 'Hello, write a Spring Boot REST controller'

REGISTER IN SUPREMEAI AS FALLBACK PROVIDER:
  Provider endpoint: http://localhost:11434/api/generate
  Model: codellama:7b    Type: REST_JSON
  Priority: set below cloud providers (only used as fallback when others fail)
  Verify: Admin > AI Providers > Ollama > Test → should return valid code

HARDWARE:
  phi-3-mini  -> 6 GB RAM minimum, 2 CPU cores
  codellama:7b -> 8 GB RAM minimum, 4 CPU cores
  With CUDA GPU -> 10x inference speed

FAILURE DIAGNOSIS: curl -s http://localhost:11434/api/tags | python3 -m json.tool

task: local ai model setup llama.cpp gguf server install build compile inference cpu gpu
solution:
[SupremeAI Core — Local AI Setup: llama.cpp]

Ultra-lightweight on-device inference with no heavy dependencies.

BUILD:
  git clone https://github.com/ggerganov/llama.cpp && cd llama.cpp
  make -j$(nproc)
  # GPU option: make LLAMA_CUDA=1 (NVIDIA) | make LLAMA_HIP=1 (AMD)

DOWNLOAD GGUF MODEL:
  wget https://huggingface.co/TheBloke/Phi-3-mini-4k-instruct-GGUF/resolve/main/phi-3-mini-4k-instruct.Q4_K_M.gguf

START SERVER:
  ./server -m phi-3-mini-4k-instruct.Q4_K_M.gguf -c 4096 --port 8081
  # Test: curl http://localhost:8081 -d request_body...

REGISTER IN SUPREMEAI:
  endpoint=http://localhost:8081   model=phi-3-mini   type=OPENAI_COMPAT

MEMORY (Q4_K_M): ~1.8 GB   SPEED: ~15-30 t/s CPU-only | ~200 t/s CUDA

QUANTIZATION NOTE: Q4_0 uses 70% of Q8_0 memory with ~2% quality drop.
For production local fallback, use Q5_K_M or Q6_K.

FAILURE: if CUDA build fails, install CUDA toolkit 11.8+, set CUDA_HOME, rebuild.

task: local ai model inference gpu cuda nvidia amd rocm apple silicon metal cpu accelerator
solution:
[SupremeAI Core — Local AI Inference: Hardware Acceleration]

GPU offloads reduces inference cost by 10-50x vs CPU-only.

NVIDIA CUDA:
  ollama run codellama:7b  # auto-detects NVIDIA if CUDA installed
  llama.cpp: make LLAMA_CUDA=1 && ./server --gpu-layers 35
  Verify: nvidia-smi (should show llama/ollama process using GPU)

AMD ROCm:
  ollama run codellama:7b  # auto-detects AMD GPU with ROCm
  llama.cpp: make LLAMA_HIP=1 && ./server --gpu-layers 35

APPLE SILICON (M1/M2/M3):
  ollama run phi-3-mini  # Metal auto-enabled; fastest local option
  Verify: ollama ps shows GPU usage if Metal active

CPU ONLY (no GPU available):
  phi-3-mini (Q4_K_M GGUF) on 8 GB RAM -> ~15 t/s
  codellama:7b on 8 GB RAM -> ~7-10 t/s

BALANCED LOCAL FALLBACK FOR SUPREMEAI:
  phi-3-mini (CPU, fast prototyping) + codellama:7b (GPU, full code generation)

task: p2p knowledge sync offline firestore down multicast knowledge broadcast share instances lan mlan
solution:
[SupremeAI Core — P2P Knowledge Sync: Offline Inter-Instance]

USE WHEN: Firestore and all cloud AI are down; 2+ SupremeAI instances on same LAN.

SETUP (do BEFORE going offline — cloud must be up for first discovery):
  1. Enable: supremeai.p2p.enabled=true in config
  2. Multicast group: supremeai.p2p.multicast-group=239.255.42.99
  3. Port: supremeai.p2p.port=54321
  4. Restart all instances — auto-discover each other via mDNS/multicast

SYNC PROTOCOL:
  1. BROADCAST: new knowledge multicast to group on every update
  2. RECEIVE: listen for knowledge broadcasts from peers
  3. VALIDATE: check SHA-256 content hash of incoming message
  4. MERGE: add to local store if confidence > existing entry
  5. DEDUPLICATE: skip if content hash matches existing entry
  6. PROPAGATE: forward to peers with TTL decrement (prevents loops)

CONFLICT RESOLUTION:
  * Same topic, different solutions  -> keep highest confidence
  * Same topic, identical solution  -> merge source lists
  * Contradictory solutions -> flag admin review
  * Always prefer user-verified over auto-generated

CLOUD RECONNECT: When Firestore restores, P2P knowledge is uploaded to Firestore with learnedFrom=P2P_SYNC. All peers updated automatically.

task: p2p knowledge sync bandwidth throttle compression large payload chunked transfer ttl loop prevent
solution:
[SupremeAI Core — P2P Sync: Bandwidth & Large Payload Management]

When syncing large knowledge bases over LAN/WAN or handling mid-sync peer drop:

COMPRESSION:
  * Reduce payload by 4x using zstd compression (default if payload > 4 MB)
  * Chunk large payloads: 4 MB per chunk (configurable in supremeai.p2p.chunk-size)

THROTTLE BY NETWORK TYPE:
  LAN (same subnet):  max throughput 110 Mb/s
  WAN (across ISP):   max throughput 25 Mb/s
  Cellular/WiFi:      max throughput 5 Mb/s

CHUNKED TRANSFER (for large payloads > chunk-size):
  1. Split payload into sequentially numbered chunks
  2. Push each chunk to queue with TTL (= max retries per peer)
  3. Acknowledge: partial sync resumes from acknowledgment point on peer reconnect
  4. Re-sync: if peer dies mid-sync, remaining peers supply missing chunks

TTL & LOOP PREVENTION:
  * Decrement TTL on each forward; drop if TTL == 0
  * Drop if content SHA-256 hash already in local cache
  * No knowledge is lost until ALL peers acknowledge receipt of all chunks

task: securityconfig permitall bypass spring security admin endpoints
solution:
[SupremeAI Core — Security Fix: permitAll() Bypass Resolution]

1. Problem: SecurityConfig had .permitAll() for admin endpoints exposing them publicly
2. Fix: Changed to .hasRole("ADMIN") for all sensitive endpoints
3. Affected patterns:
   - /api/admin/** → hasRole("ADMIN")
   - /api/self-healing/** → hasRole("ADMIN")
   - /api/healing/** → hasRole("ADMIN")
   - /api/debug/** → hasRole("ADMIN")
   - /api/security/** → hasRole("ADMIN")
   - /api/workflows/** → hasRole("ADMIN")
4. Static resources, login, and health endpoints remain permitAll()
5. CSRF: Only /api/auth/** and /ws/** are exempt
6. Verify: curl -v http://localhost:8080/api/admin/providers should return 401/403 without admin token

task: firestore rules isserviceaccount bypass security vulnerability
solution:
[SupremeAI Core — Firestore Rules Security Fix]

1. Problem: isServiceAccount() function returned true for any unauthenticated request
2. Fix: Completely removed isServiceAccount() function
3. New rules structure:
   - isAuthenticated() = request.auth != null
   - isAdmin() = authenticated + (token.admin OR user.tier == 'ADMIN')
   - isOwner(userId) = authenticated + uid match
4. Admin collections (system_configs, api_providers, vpn_connections, etc.) → isAdmin()
5. User collections → isOwner() or isAuthenticated()
6. Catch-all: allow read, write: if false (deny by default)
7. Verify: unauthenticated request to any collection should be denied
